skill.name: domain-profiles
skill.description: Domain-specific configuration profiles for creating educational learning resources across technology, history, science, arts, and general topics.
skill.summary: Configure AI agents with domain-specific research, writing, terminology, and review guidance for educational content.
skill.author: BitYoungjae

security_audit.summary: AI review found the static security findings are false positives from markdown documentation, inline field names, citations, and examples of forbidden patterns. No executable scripts, command execution paths, credential access, data exfiltration behavior, or prompt injection attempts were found in the reviewed skill files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections are markdown code fences, inline field names, and example formatting. They do not invoke a shell or define executable scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are markdown fences or inline code examples in documentation. No runtime file, shell invocation, or user-controlled command path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Forbidden eval and exec Examples
low_findings.description: The technology profile mentions eval and exec only as unsafe examples and reviewer checks. The text explicitly forbids these patterns rather than using them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is a forbidden-pattern checklist and regex examples for review. There is no dynamic code execution path in the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation URLs Flagged as Network Risk
low_findings.description: The URL detections are a Python documentation citation and a commented placeholder API URL inside an example. The skill does not perform automatic network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The references appear inside markdown documentation and a commented example line. No credential transmission, tracking endpoint, or live fetch code is executed by the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto and C2 Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and C2 detections are substring matches in ordinary educational text, examples, or prior audit output. No cryptographic implementation or command-and-control behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found natural-language matches such as descriptions, guides, and equations. No md5, sha1, DES, RC4, encrypted payload, beaconing, or C2 endpoint evidence was found.

content.user_title: Configure Domain Profiles for Learning Content
content.value_statement: Educational content often needs different research methods, terminology rules, and review checks by subject. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code reusable domain profiles for consistent learning materials.
actual_capabilities: Defines search strategies for technology, history, science, arts, and general topics.
Maps each domain to special metadata fields and source quality indicators.
Provides terminology policies for Korean and English educational writing.
Supplies content structures for tutorials, timelines, lessons, exercises, and reviews.
Gives reviewer criteria for domain-specific quality checks and revision decisions.
Shows standard profile loading patterns for researcher, writer, and reviewer agents.
limitations: It provides guidance files and does not generate finished lessons by itself.
It does not validate facts, citations, equations, or learning outcomes automatically.
It focuses on Korean and English educational content patterns.
It covers five broad domains, not every specialized academic field.

use_cases.title: Standardize multi-subject lessons
use_cases.description: Apply consistent structures while respecting subject-specific needs for sources, terminology, examples, and review criteria.

use_cases.title: Plan programming tutorials
use_cases.description: Use technology guidance for official sources, version notes, runnable examples, and unsafe code pattern checks.

use_cases.title: Prepare domain-aware source lists
use_cases.description: Select stronger search queries and quality indicators before collecting references for history, science, arts, or general topics.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a domain profile
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new learning resource
prompt_templates.prompt: Read SKILL.md and choose the best domain profile for {topic}. Explain why that profile fits the learning goal.

prompt_templates.title: Create a research plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the {domain} profile and create a source search plan for {topic}. Include query patterns and quality indicators.

prompt_templates.title: Design the lesson structure
prompt_templates.scenario: Writer planning a full educational document
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the {domain} profile to outline a lesson for {topic}. Apply its terminology policy and recommended content structure.

prompt_templates.title: Review a draft by domain
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this draft using the {domain} profile review criteria. Separate critical issues, warnings, and style improvements.

output_examples.input: Use the technology profile to plan a beginner guide for Python async programming.
output: Recommended sources include official Python documentation, release notes, and maintained community examples.
The plan includes environment setup, basic async syntax, error handling, and version notes.
Review checks include unsafe eval or exec examples, missing language labels, and unvalidated input.

output_examples.input: Use the history profile to structure a lesson about the Renaissance.
output: The outline starts with historical context, key events, primary sources, multiple perspectives, and long-term influence.
The terminology plan includes original terms, modern translations, dates, and clear citation format.
Review checks include missing citations, anachronism, unsupported interpretation, and single-perspective bias.

output_examples.input: Use the science profile to prepare material about Newtonian mechanics.
output: The lesson includes concept motivation, mathematical definitions, worked problems, applications, and practice questions.
The profile requires clear symbol definitions, SI units, equation formatting, and prerequisite notes.
Laboratory or experiment sections should include safety guidance when relevant.

best_practices: Load the matching domain profile before research, writing, or review work begins.
Use the profile terminology policy on first mention of important technical terms.
Apply the profile review criteria before publishing or handing off learning content.
anti_patterns: Using one generic lesson structure for every academic subject.
Skipping citation and source quality rules for factual or historical claims.
Ignoring domain safety checks for code examples, lab work, or art materials.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It provides markdown profiles that guide research, writing, terminology, and review for different educational domains.

faq.question: Which domains are included?
faq.answer: It includes technology, history, science, arts, and general language or education topics.

faq.question: Does it execute code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation only and uses profile files as guidance for other agents.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The profiles can guide Claude, Codex, and Claude Code when creating or reviewing learning resources.

faq.question: How should I handle mixed-domain topics?
faq.answer: Start with the closest primary domain, then borrow sections from other profiles when the topic clearly needs them.

faq.question: Is the content bilingual?
faq.answer: Many examples use Korean and English terminology rules, but the structures can support other language choices.
